User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the HoneyNotes application.

Letter.title: Letter
Letter.description: Represents a letter created within the HoneyNotes application.

senderId.description: Reference to the User who sent the letter. (Relationship: User 1:N Letter)

paperColor.description: Color of the paper used in the letter.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the letter was created.

aiFeedback.description: AI feedback on the letter content.

Recipient.title: Recipient
Recipient.description: Represents a recipient of a letter.

letterId.description: Reference to the Letter received. (Relationship: Letter 1:N Recipient)

recipientId.description: Reference to the User who received the letter. (Relationship: User 1:N Recipient)

openedTimestamp.description: Timestamp of when the letter was opened.

polenReward.description: Polen reward for completing the task.

UserTask.title: UserTask
UserTask.description: Represents a user's task completion status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user task.

userId.description: Reference to the User. (Relationship: User 1:N UserTask)

taskId.description: Reference to the Task. (Relationship: Task 1:N UserTask)

completed.description: Indicates if the user has completed the task.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. The `userId` parameter is the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores letters created by the user. The `userId` parameter is the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: letterId
params.description: The unique identifier for the letter.

definition.description: Stores recipient information for a specific letter. The `userId` parameter is the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: letterId
params.description: The unique identifier for the letter.

params.name: recipientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the recipient.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier for the task.

definition.description: Stores the completion status of tasks for each user. The `userId` parameter is the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: userTaskId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user task.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure, scalable, and easily maintainable data model for the HoneyNotes application, adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). The structure leverages path-based ownership and denormalization to avoid `get()` calls in security rules and simplify authorization logic.

1.  **Users:** User data is stored in a dedicated collection `/users/{userId}`.  This is the root for user-owned data.
2.  **Letters:** Letters are stored within the user's document using a hierarchical path `/users/{userId}/letters/{letterId}`. This clearly establishes ownership and simplifies security rules based on `request.auth.uid`. The letters collection stores the letter content, configurations, timestamps, status, and AI feedback.
3.  **Recipients:** Recipient information is stored as a subcollection `/users/{userId}/letters/{letterId}/recipients/{recipientId}`. This structure ensures that only the sender of the letter has access to recipient information for that specific letter.
4.  **Tasks:** Tasks are stored in a root-level collection `/tasks/{taskId}`. These tasks are global and not user-specific.
5.  **UserTasks:** The `UserTask` documents track the completion status of tasks for each user. They are stored in `/users/{userId}/userTasks/{userTaskId}`. This allows easy querying and management of user-specific task completion.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization):**
The structure avoids `get()` calls in security rules by using path-based ownership.  The `letters` and `userTasks` collections are subcollections of `/users/{userId}`, ensuring that the `userId` is readily available in the path for security rules. No denormalization is needed in this design, as authorization is naturally derived from the path.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   The segregation of data into user-specific subcollections enables efficient and secure `list` operations. Security rules can easily filter based on the `userId` in the path, ensuring that users can only access their own data. Global `tasks` is also a root-level collection that may need further fields (e.g. 'status') to allow filtering.
